completion_promises.evidence: Added 'pydantic-settings>=2.0' to the dependencies list in pyproject.toml alongside existing pydantic>=2.0
Created src/nats_core/config.py containing NATSConfig class with all required elements (future annotations, BaseSettings inheritance, Google-style docstring, SettingsConfigDict model_config)
First non-docstring import in config.py is `from __future__ import annotations`. Verified by test_module_has_future_annotations which inspects the source code.
NATSConfig class inherits from pydantic_settings.BaseSettings. Verified by test_nats_config_is_base_settings_subclass which asserts issubclass(NATSConfig, BaseSettings).
Module has a Google-style docstring describing NATS connection configuration. Verified by test_module_has_google_style_docstring which checks mod.__doc__ is not None and non-empty.
NATSConfig.model_config is set to SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix='NATS_', env_file='.env'). Verified by test_nats_config_has_model_config which asserts both env_prefix and env_file values.
Added NATSConfig import from nats_core.config and added 'NATSConfig' to __all__ in __init__.py. Verified by test_nats_config_importable_from_package and test_nats_config_in_all.
Ran `python3 -m pip install -e ".[dev]"` which completed successfully, installing nats-core 0.1.0 with pydantic-settings 2.12.0 resolved.
Ran `ruff check src/ tests/test_config.py` which returned 'All checks passed!' with zero errors on all new and modified files.
